project.name: pulseops
project.description: AI-powered team operations and workflow intelligence platform

nodes.name: add_indexes.py
nodes.summary: One-off maintenance script that creates performance indexes (tasks, projects, activity log) directly against the database via the async engine.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker making the v1 API directory an importable Python package.

nodes.name: ai.py
nodes.summary: Largest API router: AI-powered endpoints for intake processing, email and transcript extraction, chat, dedup checking, summaries, and priority suggestions, all backed by GPT-4o structured completions and pgvector embeddings.

nodes.name: analytics.py
nodes.summary: Analytics API router serving dashboard statistics, per-user dashboards, Gantt chart data, task balance across people, and project health lookups.

nodes.name: kanban.py
nodes.summary: Small API router handling kanban board moves: updates a project's status column with permission checks and activity logging.

nodes.name: mcp_server.py
nodes.summary: MCP (Model Context Protocol) server exposing PulseOps as agent tools: task/project CRUD, dashboards, search, and AI intake, authenticated via bearer tokens against the same database.

nodes.name: notifications.py
nodes.summary: Notifications API router for listing a user's notifications and marking one or all as read.

nodes.name: projects.py
nodes.summary: Projects API router with kanban-optimized listing (cached), filtered list/search, and CRUD endpoints guarded by ownership/role checks with activity logging.

nodes.name: proposed_tasks.py
nodes.summary: API router for AI-proposed tasks from meeting transcripts: lists and counts pending proposals and confirms/dismisses them in bulk with fuzzy duplicate-title detection and project resolution.

nodes.name: search.py
nodes.summary: Search API router combining keyword (ILIKE) search over projects and tasks with pgvector semantic search via the embedding service.

nodes.name: tasks.py
nodes.summary: Tasks API router with list/day-view/CRUD endpoints, assignee resolution by name or email, project progress recalculation, and activity logging.

nodes.name: process_intake
nodes.summary: Runs GPT-4o structured extraction over a raw intake request, classifying it as project/task and persisting a RequestIntake row for confirmation.

nodes.name: confirm_intake
nodes.summary: Confirms a processed intake: creates the project or task (resolving assignees and projects), logs activity, embeds content, and marks the intake confirmed.

nodes.name: extract_email
nodes.summary: Extracts action items from an email body via GPT-4o and stores an EmailIngestion record with the parsed tasks.

nodes.name: extract_transcript
nodes.summary: Extracts action items and a summary from a meeting transcript via GPT-4o, storing a MeetingTranscript row and embedding the content in the background.

nodes.name: transcript_feedback
nodes.summary: Records user feedback on transcript search accuracy into the TranscriptSearchLog table.

nodes.name: transcript_search_diagnostics
nodes.summary: Returns aggregate transcript search accuracy diagnostics (hit rate, recent misses) from the search log.

nodes.name: create_tasks_from_transcript
nodes.summary: Creates real tasks from a stored transcript's extracted action items, resolving assignees and recalculating project progress.

nodes.name: list_transcripts
nodes.summary: Lists recent meeting transcripts with extraction status for the transcript inbox.

nodes.name: get_transcript
nodes.summary: Fetches a single meeting transcript with its full text and extracted action items.

nodes.name: claude_chat
nodes.summary: Free-form chat endpoint that augments the LLM prompt with live project/task context pulled from the database.

nodes.name: generate_summary
nodes.summary: Generates and stores an AI summary (daily/weekly/project) from current project and task state.

nodes.name: suggest_priority
nodes.summary: Asks GPT-4o for a structured priority suggestion for a task with reasoning.

nodes.name: suggest_next_actions
nodes.summary: Suggests next actions for a project by prompting the LLM with its open tasks and status.

nodes.name: ai_chat
nodes.summary: Intent-routing chat endpoint: classifies the user message (create task, query, chat) via structured completion and executes the matching database action.

nodes.name: check_task_duplicates
nodes.summary: Checks candidate tasks against existing ones using embedding similarity plus an LLM adjudication pass, returning match verdicts.

nodes.name: apply_dedup_decisions
nodes.summary: Applies user decisions from a dedup review: creates kept tasks and skips those marked as duplicates.

nodes.name: confirm_tasks
nodes.summary: Bulk-creates confirmed tasks from an AI extraction, resolving projects and assignees and recalculating progress.

nodes.name: get_insights
nodes.summary: Lists active (non-dismissed) AI insights, optionally filtered by project.

nodes.name: dismiss_insight
nodes.summary: Marks an AI insight as dismissed so it no longer appears in the feed.

nodes.name: get_dashboard
nodes.summary: Builds the team dashboard payload: project/task counts by status and priority, overdue items, completion trends, and recent activity.

nodes.name: get_my_dashboard
nodes.summary: Builds a per-user dashboard with the current user's tasks, workload, and due-soon items.

nodes.name: get_gantt_data
nodes.summary: Produces Gantt chart rows from projects and dated tasks for the timeline view.

nodes.name: get_task_balance
nodes.summary: Computes task-count balance per person by priority to surface workload skew.

nodes.name: get_project_health
nodes.summary: Returns the latest recorded health snapshot for a project.

nodes.name: move_project
nodes.summary: Moves a project to a new kanban status column with edit-permission checks, activity logging, and kanban cache busting.

nodes.name: list_my_tasks
nodes.summary: MCP tool listing the authenticated user's tasks with status/priority/overdue filters.

nodes.name: list_my_projects
nodes.summary: MCP tool listing projects owned by or involving the authenticated user.

nodes.name: create_task

nodes.name: complete_task

nodes.name: update_task_status
nodes.summary: MCP tool moving a task to a new status by fuzzy title or id match.

nodes.name: get_project_summary
nodes.summary: MCP tool returning a project's status, progress, and open-task summary by name.

nodes.name: log_transcript_search
nodes.summary: MCP tool logging a transcript search attempt and its accuracy for diagnostics.

nodes.name: get_transcript_diagnostics
nodes.summary: MCP tool returning aggregate transcript-search accuracy stats from the log.

nodes.name: list_all_projects
nodes.summary: MCP tool listing all projects with status/priority/text filters and a result limit.

nodes.name: get_project
nodes.summary: MCP tool fetching one project's full detail by id or fuzzy name.

nodes.name: create_project
nodes.summary: MCP tool creating a project with optional priority, status, due date, and tags.

nodes.name: update_project
nodes.summary: MCP tool updating project fields (status, priority, progress, blockers, next action) by id or name.

nodes.name: move_project
nodes.summary: MCP tool moving a project to a new kanban status column.

nodes.name: get_dashboard
nodes.summary: MCP tool returning the same aggregate dashboard stats as the analytics API.

nodes.name: get_gantt
nodes.summary: MCP tool returning Gantt timeline rows for projects.

nodes.name: search_projects
nodes.summary: MCP tool running keyword search across project titles and descriptions.

nodes.name: ai_chat
nodes.summary: MCP tool proxying a message to the internal AI chat endpoint logic.

nodes.name: process_email
nodes.summary: MCP tool extracting action items from an email body via the AI extraction flow.

nodes.name: analyze_transcript
nodes.summary: MCP tool running transcript extraction (summary plus action items) through the AI pipeline.

nodes.name: ai_intake
nodes.summary: MCP tool routing raw free-text input through the AI intake classification flow.

nodes.name: list_notifications
nodes.summary: Lists the current user's notifications, newest first, with an optional unread-only filter.

nodes.name: mark_all_read
nodes.summary: Marks all of the current user's notifications as read in one update.

nodes.name: mark_notification_read
nodes.summary: Marks a single notification as read after verifying it belongs to the current user.

nodes.name: list_projects_kanban
nodes.summary: Kanban-optimized project listing with an in-process cache, task counts, and column grouping payload.

nodes.name: list_projects
nodes.summary: Filtered, paginated project listing with status/priority/owner/team/text-query filters.

nodes.name: create_project
nodes.summary: Creates a project owned by the current user and logs the creation activity.

nodes.name: get_project
nodes.summary: Fetches one project by id with eager-loaded relationships.

nodes.name: update_project
nodes.summary: Updates project fields after an ownership/role permission check, logging changed values and busting the kanban cache.

nodes.name: delete_project
nodes.summary: Deletes a project and its dependents after a permission check, logging the deletion.

nodes.name: normalize_title
nodes.summary: Normalizes a task title (lowercase, strip, collapse whitespace) for duplicate comparison.

nodes.name: is_duplicate_title
nodes.summary: Fuzzy-compares two normalized titles with a similarity threshold to flag duplicates.

nodes.name: list_proposed_tasks
nodes.summary: Lists the current user's AI-proposed tasks filtered by status (default pending).

nodes.name: count_proposed_tasks
nodes.summary: Returns the count of pending proposed tasks for the notification bell badge.

nodes.name: _resolve_target_project
nodes.summary: Resolves the project to attach confirmed tasks to: explicit request id, transcript-linked project, or the General fallback project.

nodes.name: keyword_search
nodes.summary: ILIKE keyword search across projects and tasks returning typed, ranked results.

nodes.name: semantic_search_endpoint
nodes.summary: Semantic search endpoint delegating to the pgvector embedding service with threshold and type filters.

nodes.name: recalc_project_progress
nodes.summary: Recomputes a project's progress percentage from its tasks' completion state and updates the row in its own session.

nodes.name: list_tasks
nodes.summary: Lists tasks with optional project and assignee filters, eager-loading assignee and project.

nodes.name: day_view
nodes.summary: Returns tasks grouped by day across a date range for the calendar/day view.

nodes.name: create_task
nodes.summary: Creates a task, resolving a name/email assignee string to a user id and logging activity.

nodes.name: update_task
nodes.summary: Updates task fields with permission checks, assignee re-resolution, activity logging, and project progress recalculation.

nodes.name: delete_task
nodes.summary: Deletes a task after a permission check and recalculates the parent project's progress.

nodes.name: users.py
nodes.summary: Minimal users API router exposing a list of all users for assignee pickers.

nodes.name: deps.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI dependency providers: resolves the current user from a JWT bearer token, enforces admin/writer roles, and yields role-scoped database sessions.

nodes.name: session.py
nodes.summary: Async SQLAlchemy database setup: engine creation from settings, session factory, declarative Base, and the get_db request-scoped session dependency.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI application entry point: builds the app with CORS and SSE/MCP middleware, runs idempotent startup migrations, starts the transcript-poll scheduler, mounts all v1 routers, and exposes health endpoints.

nodes.name: models.py
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy ORM data model for the whole platform: users, teams, projects, tasks, comments, activity, notifications, AI artifacts (summaries, insights, intakes), meeting transcripts, proposed tasks, email ingestion, and pgvector embeddings.

nodes.name: schemas.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic request/response schemas for every API surface: auth tokens, users, projects (including kanban-optimized output), tasks, comments, notifications, AI intake/extraction results, search, analytics, and proposed-task confirmation.

nodes.summary: Embedding service wrapping HuggingFace all-MiniLM-L6-v2: generates 384-dim vectors, stores them in the pgvector embeddings table, and runs cosine-similarity semantic search.

nodes.name: user_service.py
nodes.summary: Tiny user service that finds an existing user by fuzzy name/email match or creates a placeholder user for task assignment.

nodes.name: backfill_activity.py
nodes.summary: One-off script that backfills the activity log with synthetic created/status events for existing projects and tasks.

nodes.name: create_general.py

nodes.name: list_users
nodes.summary: Lists all users (id, name, email, role) for assignee selection.

nodes.name: get_current_user
nodes.summary: Decodes the JWT bearer token, loads the matching user (auto-provisioning on first Microsoft login), and rejects invalid or missing credentials.

nodes.name: require_admin
nodes.summary: Dependency that rejects non-admin users with 403.

nodes.name: require_writer
nodes.summary: Dependency that rejects read-only users with 403 for mutating endpoints.

nodes.name: get_db_for_user
nodes.summary: Yields a database session scoped to the current user's role for row-level behavior.

nodes.name: get_db
nodes.summary: Async generator dependency yielding a request-scoped SQLAlchemy session and ensuring it is closed.

nodes.name: SSEKeepAliveMiddleware

nodes.languageNotes: Raw ASGI middleware (not BaseHTTPMiddleware) so it can interleave keep-alive bytes into a streaming response body.

nodes.name: MCPHeaderMiddleware
nodes.summary: ASGI middleware that normalizes Accept/Content-Type headers on MCP routes so strict MCP clients interoperate with FastAPI.

nodes.name: run_migrations
nodes.summary: Startup hook running idempotent ADD COLUMN/CREATE TABLE migrations so schema changes deploy without manual SQL.

nodes.name: health_check
nodes.summary: Health endpoint verifying database connectivity for deploy checks.

nodes.name: auth.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI auth router implementing the Microsoft/MSAL OAuth login flow (login redirect, callback, one-time-code token exchange) plus the per-user Microsoft 365 Graph connect/disconnect flow that stores an encrypted MSAL token cache on the user record. Also exposes /me, MCP API-key issuance, and MCP setup-state endpoints.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses in-memory state/exchange stores with time.time() expiry for the OAuth state and one-time code exchange, and MSAL SerializableTokenCache encrypted via Fernet before persistence.

nodes.name: microsoft_login
nodes.summary: Starts the Microsoft OAuth login by generating a CSRF state token and redirecting the browser to the MSAL authorization URL.

nodes.name: microsoft_callback
nodes.summary: Handles the OAuth redirect: validates state, exchanges the auth code for tokens, fetches the user profile from Microsoft Graph, upserts the User row, then issues an app JWT via a short-lived one-time exchange code and redirects to the frontend.

nodes.name: microsoft_token
nodes.summary: Exchanges the one-time code minted during the OAuth callback for the actual JWT access token and user profile, validating expiry and decoding the embedded token.

nodes.name: get_api_key
nodes.summary: Returns the current user's MCP API key, lazily generating a URL-safe secret on first request and persisting it.

nodes.name: mcp_complete
nodes.summary: Marks the current user's MCP setup as complete and returns the updated user profile.

nodes.name: mcp_reset
nodes.summary: Resets the current user's MCP setup state and returns the updated user profile.

nodes.name: microsoft_connect
nodes.summary: Begins the per-user Microsoft 365 Graph connect flow by embedding a short-lived JWT in the OAuth state and returning the MSAL authorization URL for the connect redirect URI.

nodes.name: _m365_connect_result
nodes.summary: Renders a small self-closing HTML page that posts the M365 connect outcome back to the opener window, used as the terminal response of the connect callback.

nodes.name: microsoft_connect_callback
nodes.summary: Completes the M365 connect flow: decodes the JWT-bearing state to identify the user, exchanges the auth code with a serializable MSAL token cache, encrypts the cache with Fernet, and stores it plus a connected-at timestamp on the user.

nodes.name: microsoft_disconnect
nodes.summary: Clears the stored M365 token cache and connected-at timestamp for the current user, disconnecting their Microsoft Graph integration.

nodes.name: internal.py
nodes.summary: Internal cron-triggered API router guarded by an X-Cron-Secret header, exposing endpoints that run the reminder sweeps and the Teams transcript poll (with a 409 guard when a poll is already running).

nodes.name: trigger_reminders
nodes.summary: Cron endpoint that runs both the task due-date reminder sweep and the scheduled-block reminder sweep and returns their counts.

nodes.name: trigger_transcript_poll
nodes.summary: Cron endpoint that kicks off the Teams transcript poll, returning 409 if the poll lock is already held so overlapping runs never stack.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic Settings class centralizing all environment-driven configuration: database URL, OpenRouter and HuggingFace AI keys/models, JWT secret, CORS origins, Azure/MSAL OAuth credentials, M365 Graph scopes, token encryption key, and the cron secret.

nodes.name: Settings
nodes.summary: Settings model holding 19 configuration properties covering the database, OpenRouter/HuggingFace AI stack, JWT auth, Azure AD OAuth, M365 Graph integration, and internal cron authentication.

nodes.name: crypto.py
nodes.summary: Thin Fernet symmetric-encryption helper keyed by M365_TOKEN_ENC_KEY, used to encrypt and decrypt users' persisted MSAL token caches; raises at use time if the key is unset.

nodes.name: encrypt_str
nodes.summary: Encrypts a plaintext string with the configured Fernet key and returns the ciphertext as a string.

nodes.name: decrypt_str
nodes.summary: Decrypts a Fernet ciphertext string back to plaintext using the configured key.

nodes.name: security.py
nodes.summary: Core security helpers: bcrypt password hashing/verification and JWT access-token creation and decoding using the app SECRET_KEY and configured algorithm.

nodes.name: create_access_token
nodes.summary: Creates a signed JWT from the given claims with a configurable expiry (defaulting to ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES).

nodes.name: decode_token
nodes.summary: Decodes and validates a JWT, returning its claims or None on failure.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker for the services package; enables 'app.services' imports such as graph_service and transcript_poll_service.

nodes.name: ai_service.py
nodes.summary: OpenRouter/GPT-4o client wrapper providing structured JSON completions validated against Pydantic response models (with schema hints and markdown-fence stripping) and plain chat completions with history support.
nodes.languageNotes: Builds a compact schema hint from Pydantic model_json_schema() and injects it into the system prompt, then validates the raw JSON reply with model_validate — a lightweight alternative to native structured-output APIs.

nodes.name: structured_completion
nodes.summary: Calls the OpenRouter chat completions API in JSON mode with a schema hint derived from the target Pydantic model, strips markdown code fences from the reply, and returns a validated model instance.

nodes.name: chat_completion
nodes.summary: Runs a free-form chat completion against the configured OpenRouter model, optionally prepending conversation history, and returns the trimmed text reply.

nodes.name: graph_service.py
nodes.summary: Microsoft Graph integration service: silently refreshes per-user MSAL tokens from the encrypted cache, issues authenticated Graph requests, pages through calendar events, lists and fetches online-meeting transcripts, cleans VTT captions to speaker lines, and derives the Graph meeting id from a Teams join URL.
nodes.languageNotes: Reconstructs the Graph onlineMeeting id by base64-encoding a '1*{oid}*0**{thread}' string parsed out of the Teams meetup-join URL — a per-user pattern that avoids application-level Graph permissions.

nodes.name: acquire_user_token
nodes.summary: Decrypts the user's stored MSAL token cache, acquires a Graph access token silently (refreshing if needed), re-encrypts and persists the cache when it changed, and returns the token or None.

nodes.name: graph_request
nodes.summary: Generic authenticated Microsoft Graph HTTP request helper supporting relative paths or absolute nextLink URLs, query params, and JSON bodies via httpx.

nodes.name: list_calendar_events
nodes.summary: Fetches all calendar events in a time window via Graph calendarView, following @odata.nextLink pagination until exhausted and raising on non-200 responses.

nodes.name: list_meeting_transcripts
nodes.summary: Lists the transcripts available for a given online meeting id via the Graph onlineMeetings transcripts endpoint.

nodes.name: get_transcript_content
nodes.summary: Downloads a transcript's VTT content from Graph and returns it cleaned to plain speaker-attributed text.

nodes.name: clean_vtt
nodes.summary: Converts raw WebVTT caption text into clean 'Speaker: text' lines by dropping cue numbers/timestamps, extracting voice tags, and stripping residual markup.

nodes.name: meeting_id_from_join_url
nodes.summary: Derives the base64 Graph onlineMeeting id from a Teams meetup-join URL by parsing the thread id from the path and the organizer oid from the context query parameter; returns None on malformed input.

nodes.name: reminder_service.py
nodes.summary: Reminder sweep service creating in-app Notifications for tasks due within 24 hours and for scheduled work blocks starting within the next hour, with dedupe via last-reminded timestamps and rollback on failure.

nodes.name: run_task_reminders
nodes.summary: Finds assigned, incomplete tasks due within 24 hours for active users that have not been recently reminded, creates a Notification per task, stamps the reminder time, and commits — rolling back and re-raising on DB failure.

nodes.name: run_block_reminders
nodes.summary: Finds scheduled task blocks starting within the next hour for active assigned users, creates a Notification per block with the same dedupe/rollback pattern as the task sweep.

nodes.name: transcript_poll_service.py
nodes.summary: Teams transcript ingestion pipeline: under a global asyncio lock it iterates M365-connected users, walks their calendar events since a per-user cursor, ingests new meeting transcripts idempotently, runs one GPT-4o structured extraction attempt per transcript per tick, and fans extracted action items out into ProposedTask rows for the bell UI.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses PostgreSQL ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING upserts for idempotent transcript ingestion and an in-tick attempted_extractions set so each transcript gets at most one LLM extraction attempt per poll cycle; cursor advancement is preserved only for clean iterations.

nodes.name: truncate_transcript
nodes.summary: Truncates over-budget transcript text using a head/tail split so both the opening and closing of the meeting survive the LLM context budget.

nodes.name: parse_graph_datetime
nodes.summary: Parses Microsoft Graph datetime strings, trimming 7-digit fractional seconds to microseconds, coercing naive values to UTC, and returning None for empty or garbage input.

nodes.name: run_transcript_poll
nodes.summary: Top-level poll cycle: opens a DB session, selects all users with an M365 token cache, acquires a Graph token per user (skipping without cursor advance on failure), and delegates each connected user to _poll_user while accumulating run statistics.

nodes.name: _poll_user
nodes.summary: Per-user poll: lists calendar events since the user's cursor, derives meeting ids from Teams join URLs, ingests transcripts within each occurrence window, runs at most one extraction attempt per transcript per tick, fans out proposals, and advances the cursor only on a clean iteration.

nodes.name: _ensure_transcript_row
nodes.summary: Idempotently ingests a Graph transcript: downloads and truncates the content, then inserts a MeetingTranscript row with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING keyed on the Graph transcript id so re-polls never duplicate.

nodes.name: _extract_transcript
nodes.summary: Runs the GPT-4o structured extraction over an ingested transcript, storing action items, decisions, blockers, and attendees on the row and stamping extracted_at only on success (left null on timeout/failure for retry next tick).

nodes.name: _fan_out_proposals
nodes.summary: Creates one ProposedTask row per extracted action item for a user, skipping when the transcript is unextracted or the user already has proposals from it; zero-action-item extractions are terminal with no proposals.

nodes.name: test_reminders.py
nodes.summary: Pytest suite for the task reminder sweep, covering eligibility, completed/unassigned/inactive-user/recently-reminded exclusions, and rollback plus re-raise on DB failure, using AsyncMock-backed fake sessions.

nodes.name: test_transcript_poll.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive pytest suite (28 tests) for the transcript pipeline: VTT cleaning, join-URL parsing, truncation, Graph datetime parsing, calendar pagination, cursor advancement semantics, single-extraction-per-tick, proposal fan-out rules, extraction timeout handling, and the internal cron trigger's lock and secret guards.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Container image for the PulseOps Claude Bridge on Railway: a node:20-slim base that installs the Claude Code CLI globally plus Python for the bridge and MCP servers, then runs bridge.py headless, authenticated by a Claude subscription OAuth token rather than an API key.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-stage build with layer-cached pip installs; binds on :: because Railway's private network is IPv6-only, and sets HOME=/app so Claude Code has a writable config dir.

nodes.name: 001_rls_policies.sql
nodes.summary: Row Level Security migration protecting the tasks and projects tables: defines app_user_id()/app_user_is_admin() helpers reading the app.current_user_id session variable set by FastAPI, then creates per-operation policies (owner/assignee/creator/admin) with FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY and an admin/migration bypass when no user context is set.
nodes.languageNotes: FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY makes policies apply even to the postgres superuser; DROP POLICY IF EXISTS before each CREATE keeps the migration idempotent.

nodes.name: 002_intake_item_type.sql
nodes.summary: Idempotent migration adding the nullable suggested_item_type TEXT column to request_intake, used by the intake confirm flow to route AI-classified items as project vs task; mirrors the startup auto-migration in app/main.py run_migrations().

nodes.name: schema.sql
nodes.summary: Full PostgreSQL schema for PulseOps: 8 enum types and 17 tables covering users, teams, projects, tasks, comments, attachments, activity logs, notifications, AI health/summaries/insights, request intake, meeting transcripts, and email ingestion, plus 24 supporting indexes including full-text search on projects.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses PostgreSQL ENUM types (user_role, project_status, priority_level, etc.), UUID primary keys via uuid_generate_v4, JSONB columns for flexible AI output, and a GIN full-text index on projects. Must run after pgvector_setup.sql.

nodes.name: pgvector_setup.sql
nodes.summary: Bootstrap script enabling the vector, uuid-ossp, and pg_trgm extensions, creating the central embeddings table (384-dim vectors keyed by content_type/content_id), its IVF/lookup indexes, and a semantic_search SQL function returning cosine-similarity matches.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines a LANGUAGE sql STABLE function semantic_search(vector(384), ...) using the pgvector <=> cosine-distance operator with a similarity threshold and content-type filter. Designed to run FIRST, before schema.sql.

nodes.name: seed.sql
nodes.summary: Development-only seed data inserting demo users, teams, team members, projects, tasks, project health rows, AI insights, activity logs, and a meeting transcript, with fixed UUIDs and ON CONFLICT guards for idempotent re-runs.
nodes.languageNotes: Dev-only fixture data; per repo policy it must never be run against production.

nodes.name: 001_task_reminders.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding a last_reminded_at timestamp to tasks for hourly reminder deduplication, plus a partial index on open assigned tasks to speed the reminder scan.

nodes.name: 002_microsoft_oauth.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding the ms_oid Microsoft Azure AD identity column to users with a partial unique index, enabling Microsoft OAuth sign-in mapping.

nodes.name: 003_api_key.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding a long-lived api_key column to users with a partial unique index, used for MCP client authentication.

nodes.name: 004_task_scheduling.sql
nodes.summary: Migration for Franklin Covey time-blocking: adds scheduled_at and duration_minutes columns to tasks and a partial index on (assigned_to, scheduled_at) for scheduled-block lookups.

nodes.name: 005_block_reminders.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding block_reminded_at to tasks to stamp when a scheduled time-block's starting reminder was sent, and re-asserting last_reminded_at as a parity safeguard with migration 001.

nodes.name: 006_m365_token_cache.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding per-user Microsoft Graph token storage (m365_token_cache, encrypted at rest, plus m365_connected_at) to users so the AI Assistant reads each user's own mailbox instead of a shared token.

nodes.name: 007_proposed_tasks.sql
nodes.summary: Migration for meeting-transcript task intake: creates the proposed_tasks staging table for GPT-4o-extracted action items, adds Graph poll bookkeeping columns to meeting_transcripts (including a unique graph_transcript_id dedup index), and adds a per-user m365_last_transcript_poll cursor to users.
nodes.languageNotes: Concurrency-safe dedup is enforced by a partial UNIQUE index on graph_transcript_id rather than application-level check-then-insert; extraction success is signalled by extracted_at IS NOT NULL.

nodes.name: project_dependencies

nodes.name: comments

nodes.name: activity_logs

nodes.name: ai_summaries

tour.title: Project Overview & Setup
tour.description: PulseOps is an AI-powered team operations and workflow intelligence platform: a FastAPI backend, a Next.js frontend, and PostgreSQL with pgvector on Supabase. SETUP.md is the closest thing to a root README — it walks through creating the Supabase project, loading the SQL schema, and starting both servers, giving you the full shape of the system before you read any code.

tour.title: Backend Entry Point
tour.description: main.py is where the backend comes alive: it builds the FastAPI app with CORS and SSE/MCP middleware, runs idempotent startup migrations, launches the Teams transcript polling loop, and mounts all v1 API routers. With the highest fan-out of any code file (12 imports), it is the best single map of what the backend contains — every router you meet later in the tour is registered here.
tour.languageLesson: FastAPI startup hooks let an app run async initialization (migrations, background pollers) before serving traffic. Routers are composed with include_router, so each API domain lives in its own module while main.py stays a thin assembly point.

tour.title: Configuration, Security & Dependencies
tour.description: Three small modules underpin every request. config.py centralizes all environment-driven settings (database URL, OpenRouter/HuggingFace keys, JWT secret) in a Pydantic Settings class; security.py provides bcrypt password hashing and JWT creation/decoding; and deps.py turns those into FastAPI dependencies that resolve the current user from a bearer token and enforce admin/writer roles. deps.py alone is imported by 11 other files — nearly every router flows through it.
tour.languageLesson: FastAPI's Depends() implements dependency injection at the request level: a function like get_current_user is declared once and every endpoint that lists it as a parameter gets authentication for free, with automatic 401 responses on failure.

tour.title: Database Session & ORM Models
tour.description: These are the two most depended-upon files in the entire codebase (31 and 28 inbound edges). session.py sets up the async SQLAlchemy engine, session factory, and the get_db request-scoped dependency; models.py defines the ORM model for the whole platform — users, teams, projects, tasks, comments, notifications, and AI artifacts like summaries, insights, and proposed tasks. Understanding models.py gives you the vocabulary for everything else.
tour.languageLesson: Async SQLAlchemy uses an AsyncSession yielded from a generator dependency, so each request gets its own transaction scope that is committed or rolled back when the request ends.

tour.title: The Database Schema
tour.description: The SQL side of the models you just saw. schema.sql defines 8 enum types and 17 tables — users and tasks are the hubs, each referenced by 5 other nodes in the graph. pgvector_setup.sql enables the vector extension and creates the central embeddings table (384-dimension vectors keyed by content type and id) that powers semantic search. Numbered migration files evolve this schema incrementally, always idempotently.
tour.languageLesson: pgvector adds a vector column type to PostgreSQL plus distance operators for cosine similarity, letting the same database serve both relational queries and semantic search — no separate vector store needed.

tour.title: API Schemas & Core Routers
tour.description: schemas.py holds the Pydantic request/response contracts for every API surface, forming the boundary between the ORM models of Step 4 and the JSON the frontend consumes. The core routers build on it: tasks.py handles task CRUD with assignee resolution and project progress recalculation, projects.py manages the project lifecycle, and kanban.py handles board moves with permission checks and activity logging.

tour.title: The AI Layer
tour.description: This is the intelligence in "workflow intelligence". ai_service.py wraps OpenRouter/GPT-4o and returns structured JSON validated against Pydantic response models. embedding.py generates 384-dim vectors with HuggingFace's all-MiniLM-L6-v2 and stores them in the pgvector table from Step 5. ai.py — the largest router — exposes intake processing, email and transcript extraction, chat, dedup checking, and summaries, while search.py combines keyword ILIKE search with pgvector semantic search.
tour.languageLesson: Structured LLM output: prompting the model with a JSON schema hint and validating the response against a Pydantic model turns free-form GPT-4o text into typed, trustworthy data — failures surface as validation errors rather than silent garbage.

tour.title: M365 Auth & Transcript Pipeline
tour.description: PulseOps signs users in via Microsoft OAuth (auth.py implements the MSAL login redirect, callback, and one-time-code token exchange). That per-user Graph access feeds the platform's signature pipeline: transcript_poll_service.py walks each connected user's calendar under an asyncio lock, pulls Teams meeting transcripts, and runs GPT-4o extraction; results land in the proposed_tasks staging table, and proposed_tasks.py serves them for review with bulk confirm/dismiss and fuzzy duplicate detection.

tour.title: Frontend Foundation
tour.description: Switching to the Next.js side, three lib files mirror the backend core in importance (16-26 inbound edges each). types.ts defines the shared TypeScript domain types — User, Project, Task, ProposedTask — plus UI constant maps. api.ts is the typed axios client with a JWT request interceptor and namespaced endpoint groups matching the routers from Steps 6-8. store.ts holds Zustand global state: persisted auth, theme and sidebar preferences, and reminder scheduling.
tour.languageLesson: An axios request interceptor attaches the JWT from the store to every outgoing call in one place, so no component ever handles auth headers. Zustand's persist middleware keeps auth state across reloads, with a hydration flag to avoid flashing the login screen.

tour.title: Dashboard & Kanban UI
tour.description: The App Router pages compose the foundation into screens. layout.tsx wraps every dashboard route with the sidebar and header chrome. dashboard/page.tsx — the highest fan-out frontend file — combines a stats grid, AI insights, activity feed, and high-priority list, all fed by React Query against the api.ts client. KanbanBoard.tsx renders the drag-and-drop project board that calls the kanban move endpoint from Step 6.

tour.title: The Proposed-Tasks Bell
tour.description: Here the transcript pipeline from Step 8 surfaces in the UI. Header.tsx (11 inbound edges — it appears on every page) hosts ProposedTasksBell.tsx, which polls the proposed-tasks API, shows a pending count, and opens a review panel where users confirm or dismiss GPT-extracted action items. The colocated vitest suite verifies the bell's grouping and confirm/dismiss behavior — a good example of the project's frontend testing pattern.

tour.title: MCP Servers & Claude Bridge
tour.description: PulseOps also exposes itself to AI agents. Two standalone MCP stdio servers make the platform toolable: pulseops/server.py wraps the FastAPI backend (project/task CRUD, dashboards, search) and m365/server.py wraps Outlook, calendar, Teams transcripts, and SharePoint via Microsoft Graph. claude-settings.json shows how Claude Code launches both. bridge.py closes the loop the other way — a local FastAPI service that connects the in-app AI Assistant chat panel to a headless Claude Code CLI.
tour.languageLesson: MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers speak JSON-RPC over stdio: each declares typed tools an LLM client can call. Wrapping an existing REST API as an MCP server is a thin adapter — the tools simply forward to HTTP endpoints with an API key.

tour.title: Deployment on Railway
tour.description: Finally, how it all ships. Each service carries its own Railway config: the root railway.json starts the frontend via Nixpacks with Node 20 pinned in nixpacks.toml, backend/railway.json runs uvicorn, and the claude-bridge is the odd one out — it needs a real Dockerfile because its node:20-slim image must bundle both the Claude CLI and the Python bridge. Merging to master auto-deploys all services, tying the tour back to the running platform you set up in Step 1.
tour.languageLesson: Railway's Nixpacks builder infers the build from the repo (package.json, requirements.txt) with railway.json/toml overriding the start command per service — a Dockerfile is only needed when the runtime mixes ecosystems, as the bridge's Node-plus-Python image does.
